name: Saudi Arabia National Cybersecurity Authority Essential Cybersecurity Controls (ECC)
summary: Saudi Arabia's Essential Cybersecurity Controls are mandatory standards issued by the National Cybersecurity Authority (NCA) applicable to all government entities, government-affiliated organizations, and private sector entities that operate national critical infrastructure. The ECC provides 114 cybersecurity controls across five domains aligned with Saudi Vision 2030.
keyRequirements: Cybersecurity governance: policies, roles, responsibilities, and risk management framework
Cybersecurity defense: network security, application security, and endpoint protection
Cybersecurity resilience: business continuity, disaster recovery, and incident management
Third-party and cloud cybersecurity: vendor risk management and cloud service security
Industrial Control Systems (ICS) cybersecurity for critical infrastructure operators
Regular compliance assessments and reporting to the National Cybersecurity Authority
penalties: Non-compliance may result in NCA-directed corrective actions, suspension of government contracts, and regulatory sanctions. Critical infrastructure operators face escalated enforcement including operational restrictions. The NCA may conduct audits and mandate remediation timelines.
